title: Oklahoma Baptists for Abolishing Abortion Endorses Warren Hamilton for SD7
author: Sam Riley
orientation: On the eve of the August 25, 2020 Republican runoff for Oklahoma Senate District 7, the pastor-led coalition Oklahoma Baptists for Abolishing Abortion publicly endorsed challenger Warren Hamilton over eight-year incumbent Larry Boggs, rebuking Boggs for spreading the slander that abolitionism is a 'cult' and pointing to Hamilton's pledge to author the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act in the Senate.
audiences: Pastors deciding whether to enter politics publicly

questions.question: Who endorsed Warren Hamilton in the SD7 runoff, and what kind of organization made the endorsement?
questions.answer: Oklahoma Baptists for Abolishing Abortion, described as a pastor-led coalition of Southern Baptists in the state, publicly endorsed Warren Hamilton over incumbent Larry Boggs in the August 25, 2020 runoff for Senate District 7.
questions.quote: On Tuesday, Oklahoma Baptists for Abolishing Abortion endorsed Oklahoma Senate District 7 candidate Warren Hamilton. Oklahoma Baptist for Abolishing Abortion is a pastor-led coalition of Southern Baptists in the state.

questions.question: What concrete legislative commitment distinguishes Hamilton from Boggs on abortion?
questions.answer: Hamilton has promised to author the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act in the State Senate, while Boggs literally walked out when the bill was up for a committee vote.
questions.quote: Hamilton is an abolitionist who has promised to author the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act in the State Senate. Larry Boggs left the room when the motion was made to bring the bill out of committee.

questions.question: How does Pastor Bill Ascol frame the choice between Hamilton and Boggs to voters?
questions.answer: Ascol frames it as a choice between a 'principled statesman' and a 'political swamp creature,' urging voters to replace the eight-year incumbent with the challenger.
questions.quote: voters living in Oklahoma State Senate District 7 have a great opportunity to choose a principled statesman over a political swamp creature by voting FOR challenger Warren Hamilton over eight-year incumbent Senator Larry Boggs.

questions.question: How does the coalition respond to Boggs' suggestion that Hamilton belongs to a cult?
questions.answer: They flatly reject it, attesting that Hamilton is a member in good standing of an Oklahoma Southern Baptist congregation and calling the cult charge a libelous lie.
questions.quote: As Oklahoma Baptists we want everyone to know that Warren Hamilton is a member in good standing of an Oklahoma Southern Baptist congregation. Any suggestion that he is part of a cult would be laughable if it were not such a libelous lie.

questions.question: What does the article say about the broader pattern of calling abolitionists a cult?
questions.answer: It identifies the cult slander as a popular tactic used by pro-life Republican establishment senators in concert with local tabloids, citing Boggs' comparison of Hamilton and his supporters to Westboro Baptist as a prominent example.
questions.quote: The idea that abolitionism is a cult has become a popular slander among pro-life Republican establishment Senators and local tabloids working in concert with these politicians. Boggs has publicly slandered Hamilton by comparing him and his supporters to Westboro Baptist.

questions.question: What incident at the Haskell County GOP meeting does the article point to as the backdrop for Boggs' slander?
questions.answer: On July 14th, Boggs allegedly shoved Hamilton and threatened a supporter, shouting 'Screw you!... If you don't like, step outside and you square right up!' before resorting to slander to cover his behavior.
questions.quote: an incident at the Haskell County GOP meeting on July 14th where Boggs shoved Hamilton and threatened one of his supporters, shouting “Screw you!… If you don’t like, step outside and you square right up!”

questions.question: Why does the article say the stakes of this race extend beyond a single seat?
questions.answer: Because Boggs intends to keep regulating abortion while Hamilton intends to abolish it, the article frames continuing to elect Boggs-style senators as actively perpetuating the legal cover for abortion in Oklahoma.
questions.quote: If we continue sending men like Larry Boggs to Oklahoma City, nothing is going to change and babies will continue to be murdered under the cover of Oklahoma State law. Warren Hamilton is the type of man we need at the capitol to change that.

questions.question: What is Hamilton's stated ecclesial and theological pedigree according to the endorsement?
questions.audience: Pastors deciding whether to enter politics publicly
questions.answer: Hamilton is described as a 'convictional conservative' who has personally endorsed the Southern Baptist Resolution on Abolishing Abortion, anchoring his political stance to a denominational confessional act.
questions.quote: Warren Hamilton is a convictional conservative who has endorsed the Southern Baptist Resolution on Abolishing Abortion

questions.question: What concrete actions does the article ask SD7-area readers to take?
questions.answer: It asks readers to share the article with anyone they know in the listed SD7 towns so those voters know to support Hamilton on August 25th.
questions.quote: Share this article with anyone you know in Senate District 7 (McAlester, Stigler, Wilburton, Hartshorne, East Eufala, Coalgate, Savanna, Stuart, Kiowa, Quinton, Whitefield, Spiro, and the surrounding areas) so they know to vote for Warren Hamilton on August 25th!
